name: Henschel Hs 293
summary: The world's first operational anti-ship guided missile. A glide bomb fitted with a rocket motor, the Hs 293 was directed to its target by a radio operator in the launching aircraft. It sank or damaged numerous Allied warships in the Mediterranean and Atlantic, including the sloops HMS Egret and HMCS Athabaskan, before Allied jamming and tactical adaptations reduced its effectiveness.
